name: Chicken Pockets with Vegetables
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse mushrooms and pat them dry. Chop the mushrooms and set aside.
2. Wash spinach leaves and place them in a saucepan with just enough water to cover the leaves. Cook until barely wilted. Drain well and squeeze out excess moisture. Chop and set aside.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons of corn oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onions, mushrooms, and garlic. Sauté for about 5 minutes or until tender. Stir in oregano and spinach, then cook for another minute. Set aside to cool slightly.
4. Pound each chicken breast between two layers of waxed paper or plastic wrap with a rolling pin or heavy plate edge until it reaches about 1/4-inch thickness.
5. Spoon about one-fourth of the mushroom mixture onto the center of each flattened chicken breast. Roll up the chicken carefully and secure with wooden toothpicks. Place the rolls in a shallow baking dish.
6. Mix the dry white wine with the remaining corn oil. Pour over the chicken pieces. Bake at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for about 15 to 20 minutes, or until cooked through. Baste occasionally while baking.
7. Sprinkle chopped parsley over the chicken and serve with lemon wedges.
